Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

I don't think we need to introduce a UUID to ZFS.  Instead, can't we use the ZFS dataset GUID or similar?

Note that we don't need to rebuild the OI files in case of zfs send/recv, since this should maintain the same inode numbers (though I don't know if the dataset GUID is saved in that case).  OI Scrub only needs to run in the case of file-level backup/restore.

Abandoned

Replaced with ZFS ds_guid. Abandon it

parents.subject: LU-7585 scrub: general framework for OI scrub

commit.subject: LU-7585 zfs: FS uuid for zfs backend
commit.message:
LU-7585 zfs: FS uuid for zfs backend

The uuid is generated when reformat the zfs backend. OI scrub
will record it. Then by comparing it with the stored one, the
OI scrub can know whether the ZFS backend is resotred or not.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Change-Id: Idc462c84473115a33ba2ee3623f819b3a272772c
